title: Auriculardisia tysonii

It belongs to the genus Auriculardisia, which is a group of plants that are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Sapotaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 1,000 species. These plants are commonly known as sapote or mamey sapote, and are often found in tropical forests and woodlands. Auriculardisia tysonii is a relatively rare and poorly studied species, and more research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.
